Bash(PATH=/home/al/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.1/bin:__TRACKED_VAR__ npm run logbug -- 'Mailing-list simple filters save value=NULL: the repeater row has THREE same-named '\\''value'\\'' components \(text input / Yes-No select / tag picker, one visible per field type\) sharing one state path, and the inapplicable hidden ones clobber the chosen value at dehydration. NULL value then compiles to WHERE field IS NULL \(matches nothing on NOT NULL columns\) and the reloaded form shows '\\''Select an option'\\''. Reproduced 362 on a clean e2e stack via the real create form \(opt-in==Yes -> DB row value:null, matches:0\). Likely affects ALL value-carrying simple filters, not just opt-in — the feature is probably inert since s041. Fix shape: one dehydrating component per state path \(dehydrated\(condition\) per type, or split state paths mapped onto the value column\) + a round-trip Pest/e2e case. Housekeeping-sized.')
